name: Marine API Profiles
abstract: (Under construction) - defines and demonstrates a reusable mechanism for specialisation of OGC APIs using the Oceans Information Model (OIM) for semantic interoperability.
description:
Each building block defines a profile of a data exchange schema based on OGC API responses, mapped to the OIM using JSON-LD contexts. Common patterns are factored into reusable foundations to simplify development and maximise interoperability of such profiles, in a way which is scalable across future application requirements. 

Thus, a pilot project to monitor Jellyfish swarms using Citizen Science will reuse common patterns for Citizen Science observations, which in turn we reuse a common pattern for observations, using an underlying component of the OIM - the OGC Observations model (SOSA ontology).

bblocks.abstract: A source-level building block for HELCOM macroobservation point data within the Swedish EEZ, including provenance-aware marine observation properties and linked-data context.

bblocks.abstract: Defines the APKG-MAP application package metadata profile for software application packages, processes, parameters, temporal coverage, geometry, links, and secrets as described in the ILIAD APKG repository.

bblocks.name: OGC CoverageJSON
bblocks.abstract: Based on JavaScript Object Notation (JSON), CoverageJSON is a format for publishing spatiotemporal data to the Web. The primary design goals are simplicity, machine and human readability and efficiency. While other use cases are possible, the primary CoverageJSON use case is enabling the development of interactive visual websites that display and manipulate environmental data within a web browser.

sources.title: Coverage Implementation Schema that CoverageJSON is based on

bblocks.name: OGC Coverage Implementation Schema
bblocks.abstract: Coverages represent homogeneous collections of values located in space/time, such as spatio-temporal sensor, image, simulation, and statistics data. Common examples include 1-D timeseries, 2-D imagery, 3-D x/y/t image timeseries and x/y/z geophysical voxel models, as well as 4-D x/y/z/t climate and ocean data. Generally, coverages encompass multi-dimen­sional regular and irregular grids, point clouds, and general meshes. This Coverage Implementation Schema (CIS) specifies the OGC coverage model by establishing a concrete, interoperable, conformance-testable coverage structure. It is based on the abstract concepts of OGC Abstract Topic 6 [1] (which is identical to ISO 19123) which spec­i­fies an abstract model which is not per se interoperable – in other words, many different and incompatible implementations of the abstract model are possible. CIS, on the other hand, is interoperable in the sense that coverages can be conformance tested, regardless of their data format encoding, down to the level of single pixels or voxels.Coverages can be encoded in any suitable format (such as GML, JSON, GeoTIFF, or Net­CDF) and can be partitioned, e.g., for a time-interleaved representation. Coverages are independent from service definitions and, therefore, can be accessed through a variety of OGC services types, such as the Web Coverage Service (WCS) Standard [8]. The coverage structure can serve a wide range of coverage application domains, thereby contributing to harmon­ization and interoperability between and across these domains.

bblocks.name: OIM Biodiversity - TDWG
bblocks.abstract: Defines JSON schema properties mapped to the Taxonomic Database Working Group data model. This is based on Darwin Core.

bblocks.name: Vertical Datum Reference
bblocks.abstract: Reusable schema component encoding the vertical datum metadata required for unambiguous height referencing across land and marine domains, as mandated by FMSDI Best Practice 1 (Unified Geospatial Reference). Records datum name, epoch, CRS identifier, separation surface model, transformation method, vertical orientation convention, and vertical accuracy.

bblocks.name: OIM Coastal Feature
bblocks.abstract: OIM profile for features in the intertidal and coastal zone (the 'white ribbon'), encoding elevation with vertical datum, tidal context, data lineage, gap-fill documentation, and scalable resolution metadata. Implements FMSDI Best Practices 1 (Unified Geospatial Reference), 3 (Mind the Gap), and 5 (Scalable Resolution Management).

bblocks.name: Oceans Information Model
bblocks.abstract: Defines a composable Building Block view of the Oceans Information Model. This view breaks down the OIM into a set of clear dependencies and profiling layers on top of the underlying common Cross Domain Model

bblocks.abstract: Defines the OIM variable and indicator concept model for SEADOTS and ILIAD variable observations.

bblocks.name: ODD Protocol Description Record
bblocks.abstract: OGC API Records profile for simulation model publications using the ODD Protocol (Overview, Design concepts, Details). Provides a structured, open-ended scaffold for describing agent-based and individual-based models; domain-specific vocabularies (NERC, CF, Darwin Core, ICES) are injected at the entity and variable level by domain profiles.

sources.title: The ODD Protocol for Describing Agent-Based and Other Simulation Models: A Second Update
GeoDCAT mapping for OGC API Records

bblocks.name: Dynamic Shoreline Feature
bblocks.abstract: Feature type for tide-dependent shoreline vectors, encoding shoreline type, water level at capture, tidal datum reference, sensor platform, and spatial/temporal uncertainty. Enables multi-temporal shoreline analysis and supports FMSDI Best Practice 3 (Mind the Gap) and Best Practice 1 (Unified Geospatial Reference). Derived from TCarta FMSDI demonstrator at Hurst Spit using Planet Labs NDWI and Capella SAR data.

sources.title: TCarta D100 — Space-Based Remote Sensing for Intertidal Awareness (Hurst Spit NDWI + SAR demonstrator)
